title: The One Where Ross and Rachel Take A Break

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, Joey is taking a jar of olives out of the fridge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Monica) Hey, how much will you give me to eat this whole jar of olives?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I won't give you anything, but you'll owe me 2.95.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Done.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey. I need an atlas! I need an atlas!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why? (in a motherlike tone) Do you have a report due?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have a date with this diplomat I met while I was giving free massages outside the UN and, I don't know where his country is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, let's start with the free messages outside the UN.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!! That's my new thing. I figure bodies at peace, make peace.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! You might just get the first Nobel prize in rubbing. So what country is this guy from?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ick-neck-tree-anis..... There's a 'g' in there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where's that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: In your atlas!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't have an atlas.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, but wait I do have a globe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hold on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So Pheebs what's this guy like?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, well he's very dashing, y'know, and umm, very, very sophisticated, and he doesn't speak any English, but according to his translator, he totally gets me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Kay, here you go. (Hands her this tiny little globe.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What is this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's a globe and, a pencil sharpener.
Phoebe puts the globe right up next to her eye to try and find the country.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey, does anybody need anything copied? I'm going down to the Xerox place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay listen, just give me anything I can make two of.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, if you don't have anything to copy, why are you going down there?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, are you just going down there to gawk at that hot girl with the belly button ring again?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! You wanna come?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!
Opening Credits
The Xerox place, Chandler and Joey are waiting in line.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on Chloe! Finish up with your customer first. Come on Chloe! Come on Chloe!!

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: (to Chandler and Joey) Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, y'know what, we're having second thoughts about our copying needs. And we'll need a little more time to think about it.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Chloe, switch with me, there's some guys here that got a crush on you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) Okay, that hurt us.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Hi guys. I haven't seen you since this morning.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well ah, ........y'know.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Hey, what are you guys doing tomorrow night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Both of us? (points to Chandler and himself)

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Maybe. Does that scare ya?
They both start laughing. They look at each other, stop and step apart a little bit.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Relax. It's just Issac's D.J.-ing at the Philly. You should come.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We'll be there.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Great. I'll ah, see ya then.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, rock on. (Does the 'Hang 10' sign, then hides his face in shame.)
A Street, Phoebe is walking along with the diplomat (Sergei) and his translator (Mischa).

lines.speaker: Sergei
lines.text: (something in Russian or Polish)

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: He's says, 'Walking with you makes this strange city, feel like home.'

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me too. Although this city is my home, so. So that's dumb what I said, don't tell him I said that. Umm, you make something up. (Mischa does so and Sergei kisses her hand.) Nice, (to Mischa) thank you. This is me. Here.
Sergei goes up to her at her door and says something.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (leaning in) Your eyes are very pretty.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Mischa) Thank you, very much! Oh! (to Sergei) Thank you!
Sergei says something and leans in to kiss her, but just as he's about to....

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (leaning in) He would like to kiss you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Mischa) Okay, y'know what, you don't have to do that now. (Mischa translates that to Sergei) No-no-no-no!! Not him, you don't! (Mischa tells Sergei he can proceed and steps away) Well the moment's over.
Sergei says something and kisses her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: Oy!
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is showing Monica where Sergei's country is.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pointing to the globe) See there it is right there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! It's small.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. But Sergei said it took the Germans six weeks to get all the way across it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So you had fun, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Except for, y'know when you're on a date and you're getting along really great but the guy's translator keeps getting in the way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What is ah, Rach in her room?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, she's still at work, but she told me to tell you to call her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh what?! Is she gonna cancel on me again?! How can she do this? Doesn't she know it's our anniversary?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right ah, Ross, this is the extent of my knowledge on the subject. (holds up a notepad) Call Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's that on the bottom?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that's my doodle of a ladybug, with a top hat. (to Phoebe) She's fancy.
Ross calls Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (answering her phone at work) Hello.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, honey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, there was a disaster in shipping and I've got to get this order in. Honey, I'm so sorry, but it looks like I'm gonna be here all night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, do you, well umm, oh how about I come up there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no-no, no, honey please, I've got, I've just have so much to deal with.
[Cut to kitchen.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Monica) Anyway, I'm going out with Sergei again tonight, and um, could you come and be the translator's date? So that when we, it's time for our alone time, you two could split off. Y'know, he's really, he's kinda cute.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well kinda cute, like really kinda cute, or kinda cute like your friend Spackel Back Larry?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, don't call him that! His name is Spackel Back Harry!
Central Perk, Chandler and Joey are sitting on the big couch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, y'know what, maybe we should get going. I mean what time did Chloe say we should be there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What time is it now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah all right, so we'll hang out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Hey, remember when she brought up that thing about the three of us?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Vividly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She was kidding about that right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I-I-I think so. (Pauses and thinks about it for a second) Yeah, I-I think so...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: God, that would be weird it that situation presented itself tonight, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, I mean what, what would we do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She was kidding.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She was.... But y'know what, just in case, maybe we should come up with a set of ground rules.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, for sure. Okay. Probably want the first thing to be, never open your eyes. Y'know, because you don't want to be doing something and then look up and see something you don't want to be seeing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. Good call, nice one. Hold it!! Hold it! What if me eyes are closed, and, and my hand is out there.... (holds his arm out and pretends to grab something with his hand.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah!! Okay! Eyes open at all times! Oh, hey, how do we decide where we... (clears throat) y'know each would, (clears throat again) y'know (pause) be?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right. Right. Well ah, y'know we could flip for it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I guess, but what's like heads and what's tails?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well it you don't know that, then I don't want to do this with you.
Rachel's office, Rachel and a co-worker (Sophie) are dealing with the crisis.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on the phone) No, no, no, I'm looking at a purchase order right here and it clearly states that we ordered the Rivera bikini in a variety of sizes and colours. And.... (listens) What does it matter, what I'm wearing?! Can I please speak to your supervisor? (listens) Thank you. (to Sophie) We're holding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering, carrying a basket) Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (startled) Oh!! My God, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well you said you couldn't go out so.... (pulls the cover off of the basket)

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: You brought a picnic, oh, what a boyfriend. That's it, on Monday I start wearing make-up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross honey, this is very nice, but, but I-I got a crisis.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but I got cous-cous!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, honey, I'm sorry, I know it's our anniversary but I told you on the phone I don't have time to stop.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, you don't have to stop, I'm invisible, I'm not here. (lights a candle)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I don't, hmm... (on phone) Oh, who approved that order?! (listens) Well there is no Mark Robbinson in this office. (to Sophie) Get me Mark on the phone!

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: I love Mark. (to Ross) Do you know Mark?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, let me just check that with what I got here, all right see 038 is not the number for (Ross starts making a lot of noise with a handheld pepper grinder) this store, 038 is Atlanta. And I...(stops and looks at Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stopping grinding) Pepper?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (angrily) None for me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay sorry, whew.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, as I was saying the store number is wrong, and I'm sorry but that's... (notices a fire that Ross's candle has started) Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (putting out the fire with a squeeze bottle of water) Okay, that's a fire. Oh yeah. Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on phone) Excuse me, I'm sorry, I'm gonna have to call you back, I've got a Schemp in my office. (hangs up) (to Ross) What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry. But ah, hey, oh, somebody's off the phone, how 'bout a glass of wine by the fire, I could get it going again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross you're not listening to me, I don't have time to stop.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on Rach, you don't have what, ten minutes?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't have ten minutes!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? (to Sophie) Sophie, does she have ten minutes?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, Ross!!! I told you I don't!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don't yell at me okay, this is the most I've seen you all week.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, I cannot do this right now, okay, I've got a deadline, would you just go home, I'll talk to you later. (storms out)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but wait...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good bye!
Ross starts to pack up the picnic in anger, and throws a three hole punch in the basket.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Actually, that's our three hole punch.
Monica and Rachel's, Ross is eating the picnic as Rachel comes home from work.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. Look um, about what happened earlier...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, hey, well, I-I completely understand. You were, you were stressed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (throws her stuff down) I was gonna give you a chance to apologise to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: For what? For letting you throw me out of your office?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You had no right coming down to my office Ross. You do not bring a picnic basket to somebody's work! Unless maybe they were a park ranger!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, well excuse me for wanting to be with my girlfriend on our anniversary, boy what an ass am I.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I told you, I didn't have the time!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, well you never have the time. I mean, I don't feel like I even have a girlfriend anymore, Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wh, Ross what do you want from me? You want me, you want me to quit my job so you can feel like you have a girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but it'd be nice if you realised, it's just a job!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just a job!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross do you realise this is the first time in my life I'm doing something I actually care about. This is the first time in my life I'm doing something that I'm actually good at. I mean. if you don't get that...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, hey, I get that, okay, I get that big time. And I'm happy for ya, but I'm tired of having a relationship with your answering machine! Okay, I don't know what to do anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well neither do I!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is this about Mark?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (shocked) Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it's not, it's not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. I cannot keep having this same fight over and over again, Ross, no, you're, you're, you're making this too hard.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh I'm, I'm making this too hard. Okay, what do you want me to do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, I don't know. Urrrgh! Look, maybe we should take a break.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, fine, you're right. Let's ah, let's take a break, (goes to the door) let's cool off, okay, let's get some frozen yogart, or something.. (opens the door)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. (Ross is standing in the doorway.) A break from us.
Ross looks at her, then leaves slamming the door behind him.
Commercial Break
The Philly, Chandler and Joey are talking to Chloe.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: And the advances in collating in the past five years, I mean we just got in an X-5000, y'know. The X-5000 makes the X-50 look like a T-71.
Chandler agrees in an absolutely bored way.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: (seeing Ross enter) Hey, it's the dinosaur guy. (runs over to Ross) Hi, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, hi Chloe.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: I want you to met some friends of mine. (Introduces him to Chandler and Joey) This guy is my hero, he comes in with some stuff he wants it blown up 400%, we said we don't do that, and he says you gotta. And y'know what, we did it. And now anytime anybody wants 400, we just say 'let's Ross it!'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that's the only colour that comes in.

lines.speaker: Issac
lines.text: Yo, Chloe, do you have a quarter for the condom machine?

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Oh! (storms off)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, what are you doing here? I thought tonight was your big anniversary dinner.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, little change in plans. Ahh, we're gonna break-up instead.
Chandler and Joey stare at each other in shock.
A Restaurant, Monica and Phoebe are on their dates with Sergei and Mischa.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, and I can also speak a little French. Voulez-vous coucher avec moi ce soir? (Mischa laughs) Why? What did I say?

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: Well, you just asked if I wanted to go to bed with you tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! No wonder I get such great service at Cafe Maurice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (laughing and banging her spoon on the table) Knock, knock, knock, knock, hi. Um, could you please tell Sergei that um, I was fascinated by what Boutros Boutros Gali said in the New York Times.
Mischa does so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You didn't say Boutros Boutros Gali.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (to Sergei) Boutros Boutros Gali.
Sergei responds.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: He says he was too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Interesting.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (to Monica) So I was wondering....

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, ah, before you get all talky again, umm, could you also please tell Sergei that I really like his suit.
Mischa does so, and Sergei complements Phoebe, and says it slowly

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (to Phoebe, very quickly) Eh, he said, thank you very much, he thinks you look very pretty tonight, your hair, golden like the sun. (to Monica) So you're a chef?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm also thinking about opening up my own restaurant.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: Oh, really.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica, can I talk to you behind my menu, please. (Behind the menu) What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I was having a conversation.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah but, Mischa is so interested in you, that Sergei and I haven't been able to say two words to each other.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you want me to do? Just sit here silently while you three have a conversation?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That would be great. Thank you.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is lying in front of the bay window, and the phone rings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (jumping up to answer the phone) Hello!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Oh, hi. It's Mark.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (disappointed) Oh.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: What? Is it my breath?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Sorry, I just thought you were somebody else. Hi!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Hi. Well, look, I was just gonna leave a message, isn't tonight your, your big anniversary dinner?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Well, umm.....

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Rach, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on the verge of tears) Yeah, I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: You wanna talk, I mean I can come over?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Really, no, please, please, that's, that's okay.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: All right, all right, I'm coming over, and I'm bringing Chinese food.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah, I'm not, I'm not hungry.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: It's for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Okay, bye.
The Philly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What can I do? One person wants to break-up, you break-up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, no way! Come on, this is you guys, call her and work it out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh come on, we just had this huge fight, all right, don't I have to wait a while?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, this isn't like swimming after you eat, pick up the phone!!
Ross goes to call her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know that whole swimming thing is a myth.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, tell that to my Uncle Lenny.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why? What happened to him?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing, he's just really believes in that.
Monica and Rachel's, Mark is there, opening Chinese food boxes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, and then, we got into this big, stupid fight. I just, it was awful. I told him he treats me like a park ranger, or something, oh and then I told him I wanted to take a break, I don't want to take a break.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Wow. I'm sorry. Eggroll?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. (grabs an eggroll) And then I called him, and he wasn't there.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Well, then he's, he's probably just, out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you that's very helpful, I'm glad you came over.
The phone rings and Rachel answers it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! It's me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Oh, I'm so glad you called.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really? I've been thinking, this is crazy, I mean don't, don't you think we can work on this?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Hey, what do you want to drink?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who's that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nobody.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Is it okay if I finish the apple juice?
Rachel mouths 'Shoot!' or something similar.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is that Mark?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, honey, look he just came over to....
[Cut to The Philly.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Got it! (slams the phone down, and walks back to the bar.)

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Hey, dinosaur guy, look at you, so sad. Come dance.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, that's okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Hey, you don't have to smile. You just have to dance.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I don't feel like dancing, I feel like having a drink. Okay?

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Oh, okay. (to the bartender) Hey, two beers. (sits down next to him)
The Restaurant, after dinner.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (to Monica) And the vet said it was time. And so from half a world away, while my Mother held the phone to his ear, I said good bye to my dog,. In seven languages.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. (to Phoebe) Can I have a tissue?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, sure. I just hope you, hope you don't accidentally suck it up through your nose and choke on it.
Sergei says something to Mischa.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Sergei, would like to apologise for my behaviour tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, tell him, apology accepted.
Mischa does so.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (to Monica) Oh, he's unbelievable. I mean for the first time in three years somebody wants to actually want to talk to me, but do you think he would let me enjoy that, no!! (to Sergei) You silly diplomat, why don't you learn some English, Sergei?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Excuse me, but umm, isn't he paying for your dinner?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, the man's dog just died.
Sergei insults Mischa, and they get into a huge fight, in Russian.

lines.speaker: Mischa
lines.text: (to Monica) I have just resigned my post. Would you care to accompany me to the Rainbow Room? I have diplomatic coupons.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It will be my pleasure. (to Phoebe) My guy has diplomatic coupons. Your guy can't even say coupons. (they leave)

lines.speaker: Sergei
lines.text: Uh, (picks up a plate) plate?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Plate! Yes, plate.

lines.speaker: Sergei
lines.text: Plate. Plate. Plate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See, we don't need them.

lines.speaker: Sergei
lines.text: (picking up a cup) Plate?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.
The Philly, With or Without You is playing. (Which is the same song Ross played for Rachel in TOW the List.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Chloe) I like this song.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Well, you're practically dancing already. Why don't you just do it over here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no, no.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: What? Are you married? 'Cause that's okay.
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is next to the bay window again, Mark has left.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (picks up the phone and calls Ross) Oh, be home, be home, be home, be home, be home, be home. Be home. Be home, be home, be home. Oh, you're not home.
The Philly, Ross is dancing with Chloe.

lines.speaker: Chloe
lines.text: Still no smile?
She kisses him. Ross leans back for a second, and then they both kiss, more passionately this time as U2's With or Without You plays in the background.
To Be Continued......
Closing Credits
Central Perk, Sergei is teaching Phoebe how to sing American Pie, by Don Maclean.
Both Phoebe and him are singing along and they get to the chorus.

lines.speaker: Sergei
lines.text: (singing) Touchet, touchet, Miss Americccan pie. (stops to correct Phoebe) Ameri-ccan.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ameri-can.

lines.speaker: Sergei
lines.text: Ameri-ccan.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ameri-can. Y'know it's a very hard language. Let's do it again.
They start singing, and Phoebe covers her mouth at the American part.

lines.speaker: Sergei
lines.text: (to the rest of Central Perk) Everybody!! (In his language, and continues.)
End
